input: Survey recent papers on graph neural networks for drug discovery. Compare 5 papers and use BibTeX format.
rationale: The request explicitly asks for a bounded comparison across multiple papers.
expected_output: Should include: (1) identification of at least 5 recent papers on GNN-based drug discovery, (2) cross-paper comparison identifying shared methods and divergent findings, (3) BibTeX-formatted citations for all papers, (4) thematic synthesis rather than per-paper listing.

input: What does the literature say about RLHF? Synthesize 3 representative papers and use APA format.
rationale: The literature plus a three-paper synthesis requires cross-paper review.
expected_output: Should include: (1) identification of at least 3 representative RLHF papers, (2) thematic synthesis across papers (not sequential listing), (3) APA-formatted in-text citations and references, (4) identification of trends, tensions, or open questions in the literature.

input: Survey transformer attention variants published in the last two years on arXiv cs.CL.
rationale: A time-bounded survey across publications is a literature review.

input: What methods do recent papers use for few-shot learning in vision and language? Give me 15 papers in BibTeX.
rationale: The request asks for methods synthesized across a specified paper set.

input: Review the literature on retrieval-augmented generation, focusing on key findings, limitations, and open questions.
rationale: The request explicitly requires thematic synthesis across a literature body.

input: Compare evaluation frameworks used across LLM hallucination detection papers.
rationale: Cross-paper framework comparison requires multi-paper synthesis.

input: Summarize recent work on Monte Carlo methods for mortgage risk from the last three years.
rationale: Recent work over a time window denotes a research-literature survey.

input: Create an annotated bibliography on agentic tool use covering 20 papers in IEEE format.
rationale: An annotated bibliography across twenty papers is a literature-review task.

input: Review this paper and assess its methodology, contribution, strengths, and weaknesses: https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.06825
rationale: One specified arXiv paper requires depth-first paper review.
expected_output: Should include: (1) summary of the paper's main claims, (2) assessment of methodology (experimental design, validity), (3) evaluation of contribution significance, (4) identified strengths and weaknesses grounded in paper content, (5) constructive suggestions where applicable.

input: I attached one research paper. Summarize its method and critique the evidence supporting its conclusions.
rationale: A single attached paper requires paper review rather than a survey.

input: Analyze the experimental methodology in this preprint: https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762
rationale: The request targets methodology in one identified preprint.

input: Write a constructive peer review for the single manuscript at https://arxiv.org/abs/2203.02155
rationale: A peer review of one manuscript belongs to single-paper review.

input: Summarize this study and tell me whether its conclusions follow from the reported results.
rationale: The demonstrative reference to one study requests analysis of a single work.

input: Explain the main contribution and limitations of the paper Attention Is All You Need.
rationale: A named single paper requires focused review.

input: Explain the difference between precision and recall with one simple example.
rationale: A direct conceptual explanation requires neither academic skill.
expected_output: Should include: (1) definition of precision (TP/(TP+FP)), (2) definition of recall (TP/(TP+FN)), (3) one concrete example illustrating both metrics, (4) explanation of the trade-off between them.

input: What is attention in transformers?
rationale: A factual concept question does not request paper analysis or synthesis.

input: Search for the latest news about AI regulation and summarize the top developments.
rationale: General news research is not academic paper review or literature synthesis.

input: Write a Python function that parses BibTeX files into dictionaries.
rationale: A coding request involving BibTeX is not an academic review task.

input: Translate this paragraph to Chinese: Machine learning systems should be evaluated carefully.
rationale: Translation requires neither candidate skill.

input: Find me one good introductory paper on reinforcement learning.
rationale: Finding one recommendation is not reviewing a supplied paper or synthesizing a literature set.
